message:
So last weekend I was reorganizing our go-bags, and my 8-year-old watched me pull out snacks, a flashlight, and some first-aid stuff. She goes, 'Why are you packing like we're going camping but… not going camping?' 😄

Turns out, she'd totally forgotten we'd talked about emergency bags months ago. And that got me thinking—readiness isn't just about *having* stuff. It's also about making sure the people you love actually *know* what's in there and why it matters.

Now she helps me do a little refresh every few months. She calls it 'the boring adventure checklist,' and honestly? It's way less stressful when everyone's in on it.

Does anyone else find that involving your family (even the reluctant ones) actually makes prep feel less like a chore and more like something you're all doing together?
